items.title: Home Broadband Is 5G’s Surprise Killer App
items.summary: 5G telecommunications, according to industry hype when 5G first launched in 2019 , was going to be all about buzzy applications like mobile augmented reality and autonomous vehicles . But the surprise plot twist came when replacing home cable internet turned into 5G’s most widely adopted new application. Fixed...

items.title: The Hidden 6G Bottleneck: RF Hardware Design Is Becoming a Strategic Race
items.summary: As 5G-Advanced matures and 6G research moves closer to implementation, the wireless industry faces a deeper challenge than spectrum, standards or AI-native network architecture. Future wireless systems will depend on whether the industry can design, validate and manufacture increasingly complex RF modules fast enough.

items.title: 6G in Dalian: What the Latest 3GPP Meetings Reveal About the Future Radio and Network
items.summary: The 6G physical layer is starting to converge. The protocol stack is being simplified in meaningful places. But the most consequential architecture decisions are now moving toward the June plenary in Singapore.

items.title: one6G Supports IEEE Communications Standards Magazine Special Issue on empowering robotics with 6G
items.summary: one6G is pleased to highlight the IEEE Communications Standards Magazine special issue, “Empowering Robotics With 6G: Connectivity, Intelligence, and Beyond.” Guest-edited by Mona Ghassemian, Kaspar Althoefer, Aryan Kaushik, Mahdi Tavakoli, Douwe Dresscher, and Xueli An, this publication […] The post one6G Supports...

items.title: Satellite IoT Contenders Are Racing Against a 6G Deadline
items.summary: The decades-old “ satellite-IoT” race , in which Internet of Things devices connect to satellites in orbit, has reached an inflection point in 2026. Satellite-IoT is growing in response to a rising need for connected devices in applications including livestock tracking , wildfire detection , global supply-chain...

items.title: Direct-to-Cell Technology: Enabling Satellite Connectivity for Legacy Devices
items.summary: Direct-to-cell technology uses LEO satellites as spaceborne cell towers. It delivers LTE services to existing smartphones without hardware changes, bridging global coverage gaps. What Attendees will Learn How DTC works as a spaceborne cell tower — LEO satellites carry LTE eNodeB payloads in regenerative mode. How they...

items.title: one6G at IEEE ICC 2026: AI-Native Robotics Panel Catalyses the Evolution of 6G Telecom Networks
items.summary: At the recent IEEE International Conference on Communications (ICC 2026) in Glasgow, Scotland, the industry panel “PNL-06: AI-Native Robotics: Catalysing the Evolution of 6G Telecom Networks“ brought together leading minds to explore the deep convergence of artificial […] The post one6G at IEEE ICC 2026: AI-Native...

items.title: Deutsche Telekom and Ericsson deploy private 5G network at Hamburg port
items.summary: Covering an area of one square kilometer, the network provides state-of-the-art mobile connectivity and supports critical logistics processes as well as real-time applications.

items.title: Analysis: Starlink is helping close the U.S. digital divide
items.summary: The broadband industry should monitor the situation closely and not be like cable, which poo-pooed the threat from fixed wireless access (FWA), until it started eating into their revenues.

items.title: CBRS enjoys private network momentum: SNS
items.summary: CBRS-based private networks are drawing steady investment, with SNS Telecom projecting continued growth.

items.title: Nokia and Taiwan Mobile extend 5G partnership for AI-powered networks
items.summary: Nokia will deploy its latest AirScale portfolio, including next-generation baseband and radio solutions, alongside advanced software capabilities to enhance Taiwan Mobile’s existing network infrastructure and enable new 5G services and monetization opportunities.

items.title: Verizon puts 5G in the driver’s seat with BMW deal
items.summary: BMW vehicles will be the first to use Verizon’s Standalone 5G network.

items.title: Cisco bets on Wi-Fi–5G convergence
items.summary: Wi-Fi carries the traffic; 5G covers the miles. Cisco's wireless CTO explains why the two are "friends," how AI is pushing nurses and construction crews online full time and more.

items.title: ISAC POC targets public safety use cases with drone defense
items.summary: The weaponization of drones adds a new dimension to security and public safety. As agencies prepare, some are looking to wireless network operators as potential partners.

items.title: The Shift Toward Proactive Broadband Maintenance
items.summary: What if your network could identify problems before customers notice them? Learn how AI and machine learning are helping broadband operators improve visibility, reduce repair times and move toward proactive network management.

items.title: Inside AT&T's industrial IoT logistics play
items.summary: AT&T is leveraging its Wiliot partnership to build its logistics business. And connectivity is just part of it.

items.title: T-Mobile's network chief has a different take on autonomous networks
items.summary: T-Mobile Chief Network Officer Ankur Kapoor says efforts that focus on vertical autonomy are doing things all wrong.

items.title: Ericsson reports 2Q 2026 results
items.summary: Adjusted gross margin of 48.4%, supported by solid operational execution and improved margins in Mobile Networks.

items.title: SoftBank's AI ambitions take shape around power, compute and cloud
items.summary: Rather than competing solely on access to GPUs, SoftBank believes its new neocloud venture, SB Neo will have an advantage thanks to access to 10GW AI and energy infrastructure.
